title_en: Tafsir by the Sunnah and Companion Statements
title_id: Tafsir Berdasarkan Sunnah dan Pernyataan Sahabat
content_en:
After the Quran's interpretation of itself, the next most authoritative source for tafsir is the prophetic Sunnah, followed by the statements of the Companions (Sahabah) and their successors (Tabi'un). Ibn Taymiyyah's discussion of these sources is not merely methodological but constitutes an argument for the indispensability of the transmitted tradition in Quranic interpretation — a tradition without which the Quran's meanings would be indefinitely debated rather than reliably transmitted.

The Prophet's function as interpreter of the Quran was explicitly established by the Quran itself. Allah says: 'And We have sent down to you the Reminder that you may make clear to the people what was sent down to them' (16:44). This verse assigns the Prophet not the role of passive transmitter but of active explainer. His explanations — preserved in the Sunnah — are therefore part of the divine guidance, not external commentary. When the Prophet explained a verse, his explanation carries authority that cannot be overridden by any subsequent scholar's independent reasoning.

Ibn Taymiyyah provides classic examples of prophetic tafsir. When the verse 'Prepare against them whatever you are able of power and of steeds of war' (8:60) was revealed, the Prophet explained that 'power' (quwwah) refers to archery — an interpretation that grounds the general command in a specific application the Prophet was uniquely positioned to provide. Similarly, when the verse mentioned 'clouds over them' and 'darkness upon darkness,' the Prophet's explanations of these as referring to the doubts of the disbeliever provided interpretive keys unavailable from the text alone.

The Companions deserve special status as interpreters because they received the Quran directly from the Prophet in a living context. They knew the occasions of revelation, the pre-Islamic background of specific terms, the Prophet's explanations of specific verses, and the whole cultural and historical setting in which the revelation occurred. This knowledge is not available to any later interpreter however learned, which is why the Companions' interpretive statements carry near-prophetic authority in classical scholarship.

Among the Companions, Ibn Abbas — described in the Sunnah as the 'interpreter of the Quran' — is the most quoted exegete. The Prophet made dua for him: 'O Allah, give him deep understanding of the religion and teach him interpretation.' Ibn Abbas's explanations, transmitted through chains of varying strength, form the backbone of nearly every major classical tafsir work. Ibn Taymiyyah discusses the methodology of evaluating chains of narration in Ibn Abbas's tafsir, noting that just as in hadith, the chains must be evaluated for reliability.

The Tabi'un — the generation after the Companions — occupy the third level in this hierarchy. Their interpretations, while not carrying the authority of the Companions, represent the next best access to the early understanding of the revelation. Scholars like Mujahid ibn Jabr, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and 'Ikrimah studied directly under senior Companions and produced detailed interpretive traditions that became the raw material for the great classical tafsir works of later centuries.
content_id:
Setelah penafsiran Al-Quran dengan Al-Quran itu sendiri, sumber paling otoritatif berikutnya untuk tafsir adalah Sunnah Nabi, diikuti oleh pernyataan-pernyataan para Sahabat dan para penerus mereka (Tabi'in). Pembahasan Ibnu Taimiyyah tentang sumber-sumber ini bukan sekadar metodologis, melainkan merupakan argumen tentang keniscayaan tradisi yang diriwayatkan dalam penafsiran Al-Quran — tradisi yang tanpanya makna-makna Al-Quran akan diperdebatkan tanpa batas daripada diteruskan secara dapat diandalkan.

Fungsi Nabi sebagai penafsir Al-Quran ditetapkan secara eksplisit oleh Al-Quran itu sendiri. Allah berfirman: 'Dan Kami turunkan kepadamu Al-Quran agar kamu menerangkan kepada umat manusia apa yang telah diturunkan kepada mereka' (16:44). Ayat ini tidak memberikan Nabi peran sebagai penyampai pasif melainkan sebagai penjelas yang aktif. Penjelasan-penjelasan beliau — yang tersimpan dalam Sunnah — karenanya merupakan bagian dari bimbingan ilahi, bukan komentar eksternal. Ketika Nabi ﷺ menjelaskan sebuah ayat, penjelasannya membawa otoritas yang tidak dapat diabaikan oleh penalaran independen ulama mana pun setelahnya.

Ibnu Taimiyyah memberikan contoh-contoh klasik tafsir Nabi. Ketika ayat 'Siapkanlah untuk menghadapi mereka kekuatan apa saja yang kamu sanggupi dan dari kuda-kuda yang ditambat untuk berperang' (8:60) diturunkan, Nabi menjelaskan bahwa 'kekuatan' (quwwah) merujuk pada memanah — penafsiran yang mengakarkan perintah umum dalam penerapan spesifik yang secara unik dapat diberikan oleh Nabi. Demikian pula, ketika ayat menyebutkan 'awan di atas mereka' dan 'kegelapan yang berlapis-lapis,' penjelasan Nabi tentang hal-hal ini sebagai merujuk pada keraguan orang kafir memberikan kunci penafsiran yang tidak tersedia dari teks semata.

Para Sahabat berhak mendapatkan status khusus sebagai penafsir karena mereka menerima Al-Quran langsung dari Nabi ﷺ dalam konteks yang hidup. Mereka mengetahui asbabun nuzul, latar belakang pra-Islam dari istilah-istilah tertentu, penjelasan-penjelasan Nabi tentang ayat-ayat tertentu, dan seluruh latar budaya dan historis di mana wahyu itu terjadi. Pengetahuan ini tidak tersedia bagi penafsir mana pun di kemudian hari betapapun berpengetahuannya, itulah sebabnya pernyataan-pernyataan penafsiran Sahabat membawa otoritas yang hampir setara kenabian dalam keilmuan klasik.

Di antara para Sahabat, Ibnu Abbas — yang digambarkan dalam Sunnah sebagai 'penafsir Al-Quran' — adalah mufasir yang paling banyak dikutip. Nabi berdoa untuknya: 'Ya Allah, berilah dia pemahaman mendalam tentang agama dan ajarilah dia tafsir.' Penjelasan-penjelasan Ibnu Abbas, yang diteruskan melalui sanad-sanad yang kekuatannya bervariasi, membentuk tulang punggung hampir setiap karya tafsir klasik besar. Ibnu Taimiyyah mendiskusikan metodologi evaluasi sanad-sanad riwayat dalam tafsir Ibnu Abbas, mencatat bahwa seperti dalam hadis, sanad-sanad harus dievaluasi keandalannya.

Tabi'in — generasi setelah para Sahabat — menempati level ketiga dalam hierarki ini. Penafsiran-penafsiran mereka, meskipun tidak membawa otoritas Sahabat, mewakili akses terbaik berikutnya terhadap pemahaman awal tentang wahyu. Ulama-ulama seperti Mujahid ibn Jabr, Sa'id ibn Jubair, dan 'Ikrimah belajar langsung di bawah Sahabat senior dan menghasilkan tradisi-tradisi penafsiran yang terperinci yang menjadi bahan baku bagi karya-karya tafsir klasik besar pada abad-abad berikutnya.
content_ar: بعد the القرآن الكريم's interpretation of itself, the next most المعتمد source for التفسير is the prophetic السنة, followed by the statements of the الصحابة (الصحابة) and their التابعين (Tabi'un). ابن تيمية's discussion of these sources is not merely methodological but constitutes an argument for the indispensability of the transmitted التراث in القرآني interpretation — a التراث without which the القرآن الكريم's meanings would be indefinitely debated rather than reliably transmitted. النبي ﷺ's function as interpreter of the القرآن الكريم was explicitly established by the القرآن الكريم itself. الله says: 'And We have sent down to you the Reminder that you may make clear to the people what was sent down to them' (16:44). This الآية assigns النبي ﷺ not the role of passive transmitter but of active explainer. His explanations — preserved in the السنة — are لذلك part of the divine الهداية, not external commentary. عندما النبي ﷺ explained a الآية, his explanation carries authority that cannot be overridden by any subsequent scholar's independent reasoning. ابن تيمية provides classic examples of prophetic التفسير. عندما the الآية 'Prepare against them whatever you are able of power and of steeds of war' (8:60) was revealed, النبي ﷺ explained that 'power' (quwwah) refers to archery — an interpretation that grounds the general command in a specific application النبي ﷺ was uniquely positioned to provide. Similarly, عندما the الآية mentioned 'clouds over them' and 'darkness upon darkness,' النبي ﷺ's explanations of these as referring to the doubts of the disbeliever provided interpretive keys unavailable from the text alone. The الصحابة deserve special status as interpreters لأن they received the القرآن الكريم directly from النبي ﷺ in a living context. They knew the occasions of الوحي, the pre-Islamic background of specific terms, النبي ﷺ's explanations of specific الآيات, and the whole cultural and historical setting in which the الوحي occurred. This العلم is not available to any later interpreter غير أن learned, which is why the الصحابة' interpretive statements carry near-prophetic authority in الكلاسيكي scholarship. Among the الصحابة, ابن عباس — described in the السنة as the 'interpreter of the القرآن الكريم' — is the most quoted exegete. النبي ﷺ made الدعاء for him: 'O الله, give him deep الفهم of the religion and teach him interpretation.' ابن عباس's explanations, transmitted through chains of varying القوة, form the backbone of nearly every major الكلاسيكي التفسير work. ابن تيمية discusses the methodology of evaluating chains of narration in ابن عباس's التفسير, noting that just as in الحديث, the chains must be evaluated for reliability. The Tabi'un — the generation بعد the الصحابة — occupy the ثالثاً level in this hierarchy. Their interpretations, بينما not carrying the authority of the الصحابة, represent the next best access to the early underst
